title: An Appeal.
content:
Oh, is there not one maiden breast
    Which does not feel the moral beauty
    Of making worldly interest
    Subordinate to sense of duly?
    Who would not give up willingly
    All matrimonial ambition,
    To rescue such a one as I
    From his unfortunate position?

    Oh, is there not one maiden here,
    Whose homely face and bad complexion
    Have caused all hopes to disappear
    Of ever winning man's affection?
    To such a one, if such there be,
    I swear by Heaven's arch above you,
    If you will cast your eyes on me, 
    However plain you be I'll love you!
meaning:
"An Appeal." is a quintessential example of William Schwenck Gilbert's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
